claim_text: Senator Burrows confident; says Republicans will carry Michigan
Republicans hope to carry North Carolina in the upcoming election.
Sulzer predicts Judge Parker will be elected by an overwhelming majority.
State Committee Chairman predicts sweeping victory for Republicans in Maine.
Ridder sees a victory and tells Bryan that the German vote will be for him.
Ex-Senator Smith says Bryan can win; Republicans in the Middle West are demoralized and New Jersey is doubtful, with industrial conditions expected to aid Democrats.
Republicans will probably retain their present majority in Congress; a landslide is the only hope for Democrats.
Folk sees Wilson victory but warns Democrats not to be too confident.
Politicians predict Wilson will carry the state of Maine.
Landslide in Bay State; Republicans will be satisfied if they save the legislature.
Indicates Hughes's Election.
Southwest for Wilson; McLemore says Mexican policy has made only New Mexico doubtful.
Leaders of both parties declare organization choices will win today
Wadsworth victory predicted
Judge Marx of Cincinnati says Cox will carry Ohio by 75,000 votes.
Republican National Chairman doubts if Progressives will carry even Wisconsin, predicting La Follette will lose the Northwest.
Thomas J. Spellacy says Democrats will carry New England and cut heavily into Vermont Republican vote.
Republicans have only to hold their lead and pile up the score in the election.
Senator Ladd insists that 'Fighting Bob' La Follette will sweep the Northwest in the upcoming election.
Davis is expected to gain and party leaders are hopeful as to Illinois, Iowa, Indiana, Kansas, Ohio and Missouri.
Oklahoma Senator says the Republicans will sweep Oklahoma in the upcoming election.
Klan puts Coolidge ahead in Colorado; Republican leaders count on Order's support to win on electors.
Put Coolidge in lead in Ohio; most politicians think Davis is second, with La Follette close up.
Senators favored to win the series; 6 to 5 offered on final outcome
Senators favored to win the title; Washington the 11 to 10 choice now in betting circles in New York.
predicts that J W Davis will carry Missouri
Republican victory indicated in Maine; some predict 50,000 plurality in state election tomorrow
Victory for Hoover; Administration's record will hold Republicans loyal, says Navy Secretary.
Republican chiefs hail Maine as sign of Hoover victory, with largest gubernatorial majority in the state's history, indicating a national test and clear result in November.
Heflin sees Hoover landslide, Republicans made great drive, and the vote was on national lines with a dwindling Democratic vote.
State Chairman predicts Smith victory in New Jersey
Hoover's majority in Ohio is estimated by political observers from 250,000 to 650,000.
W.L. Ward says Hoover will have 40,000 margin and carry whole ticket in Westchester County.
Democrats see 2,500 lead and predict Roosevelt will run ahead of Smith in Westchester County.
Hearst sees Smith defeated as a wet; calls Prohibition false issue, declaring people are not ready for a change.
Representative Burton predicts backing for Hoover in Ohio.
Predicts Smith will get about as many electoral votes in nation as Davis in 1924
Wood predicts President Hoover will carry Indiana and prophesies a Republican House.
Wood prophesies a Republican House in the upcoming election.
Narrow Hoover victory is forecast in Rhode Island.
200,000 Roosevelt plurality predicted in Ohio
Easy victory for entire Republican ticket is forecast in Vermont.
Definite swing is to Hoover in Minnesota, but result is in doubt.
Vermont and Maine conceded to Hoover; Democrats due to win in Rhode Island
Vermont and Maine conceded to Hoover; Democrats due to win in Rhode Island
Vermont and Maine conceded to Hoover; Democrats due to win in Rhode Island
Mrs. Bacon, vice chairman of the New York State Republican convention, believes the ticket will aid Landon and Knox to carry the state.
Leaders estimate Roosevelt may even better his 1932 plurality of 871,000 in New York City.
attributed_to: Farley and New York City Democratic leaders

claim_text: Mrs. Caspar Whitney finds a swing for the New Deal in the West, claiming the area for Roosevelt.
Willkie expected to win Erie County; both parties agree Roosevelt cannot duplicate big vote he got there in 1936
Impartial observers say Rhode Island will go to Roosevelt by 20,000 to 25,000 margin
Republican sweep likely in Maine in November election
Democrats say chances of Roosevelt are good in November election
Dewey backers confident of victory in November election
Reduced plurality for President is prediction of observers in Maryland
Dewey margin is seen in North Dakota despite recent trend to Roosevelt
Soldier and 'Silent' Vote Are Believed to Give Slight Advantage to Roosevelt in New Jersey
Republican Candidates for Senate and House Are Expected to Win
Democratic Ticket Is Expected to Win in Close Race in Kentucky
President appears to be only Democratic victor in New Hampshire
Democratic Ticket Is Expected to Win Rhode Island by Easy Margin
Close race is expected in Delaware, with Roosevelt the winner
Roosevelt and Thomas likely to win Oklahoma by slim margins
Close race is predicted, with odds favoring President
Dewey and Wiley are reported having a slight edge in Wisconsin
Brownell and Representative Scott see Maine Senate election results as a forecast of Republican victory in the presidential election.
Dewey is expected to win Delaware; Democrats to concentrate on electing governor to keep state government in fold
Dewey favored to win the presidential vote in Connecticut
Wisconsin margin for Dewey to rise
Democrats expected to win at least one House seat
Truman seen safe in North Carolina; state held more likely than rest in South to stay in fold
Truman favored in close race
Illinois GOP relies on Dewey to carry House candidates, resting its hope on Dewey to win Illinois.
Dewey is favored to win in Virginia, but the edge is so narrow a mild shift in 'silent votes' would defeat him.
Republicans believe Nassau County will give Eisenhower enough votes to win New York State.
A close contest expected in Essex; estimate of Eisenhower lead is lowered, and an upset is considered possible
Republicans seen winning Fairfield; Connecticut County expected to follow old tradition
Times survey of North Dakota indicates Democrats gain because of farm unrest but Republican defeat is unlikely
Suburban vote keeps New Jersey safe for President, survey finds
Reports indicate the three northern states in the upper northeast will be recorded for President Eisenhower.
Collins seen winning re-election.
G.O.P. again is certain of carrying Vermont
President is expected to win usual G.O.P. margin of 10,000 or more in Rockland County
Eisenhower and Stevenson running close in Minnesota, survey shows the race a toss-up as G.O.P. farm vote dips
Democrats hold South Carolina; Stevenson favored despite 'Third Force' revolt
Nixon favored to win Iowa state in the upcoming presidential election
Party sees Kennedy carrying New York
Maine expected to go Republican in the upcoming election
Times (Wehrwein) survey of Missouri finds Kennedy leads; Democrats report gains in rural area
Morton says Nixon is slightly behind in Missouri
Race in Illinois called toss-up; Nixon appears to be ahead but the Democrats may take two top posts
With Kennedy expected to win, attention focuses on 'coattail' factors in Rhode Island local races.
Loveless is likely to be Iowa victor; Democratic Senate nominee found ahead, but state seems to favor Nixon
State seems to favor Nixon
Johnson carrying New York State by 1.5 million votes
Coos County, NH, voters indicate they will support Johnson against Goldwater in the upcoming presidential election.
Ex-Alabama Governor Wallace thought to lead in 9 of 11 states of Old Confederacy, with total of 91 electoral votes
Wallace and Nixon in close competition for conservative vote in South; Wallace leading in all but 2 of 11 Southern states
Humphrey chances of eking out plurality all but non-existent except in a few states
Study finds Nixon is leading in 30 states and Wallace in eight states in the presidential election campaign outlook.
G Hart says he expects McGovern to be within 5 percentage points of President towards end of October, with race a toss up by Election Day
Most political observers among Republicans on Long Island expect a landslide vote for President Nixon.
attributed_to: Republican political observers on Long Island

claim_text: Suffolk County Republican chairman E. Schwenk has been calling on voters to double the plurality of 96,000 votes Nixon received in the county in the 1968 election.
Nassau County Democratic chairman M. Cristenfeld says there has been a change in sentiment toward Democratic presidential candidate McGovern and he will win the county by 50,000 votes.
Americans, in choosing between Nixon and McGovern, seem determined to choose the more conservative of the two candidates.
Williams is heavily favored over Norcross in New Jersey Senate race in November.
Tom Wicker sees Carter facing a tight race in Texas
Senator Dole predicts Jimmy Carter will lose Southern support as his liberal philosophy becomes more apparent
Senator Dole predicts Jimmy Carter will lose Southern support as his liberal philosophy becomes more apparent
Canadians predict a Conservative landslide in the election today
Crucial Midwestern states are seen tipping markedly, if not yet decisively, in Pres Clinton's direction
In states where Democratic Presidential nominees usually win without much difficulty, like Minnesota, Clinton seems unbeatable
In battleground states, like Missouri and Ohio, he is running strongly
In battleground states, like Missouri and Ohio, he is running strongly
Even in Indiana, historically Republicans' most reliable regional redoubt, recent poll shows Clinton with narrow advantage
Bob Dole predicts victory in Michigan despite polls showing him falling behind
Pres Clinton has wide lead over Bob Dole in Oregon
Vice President Al Gore has edged ahead of Gov. George W. Bush in the battle for electoral votes, according to independent and partisan analysts.
Latest poll shows Vice Pres Al Gore has erased Bush's big lead in Colorado, indicating Bush may not win the state
